about.title: About Nagham
about.intro: Nagham is an interactive web application for visualizing and studying musical notes and chords through three synchronized views: staff (stave), piano keyboard, and guitar fretboard. The project was created to glorify God through music and aims to evolve into a complete music theory platform.

meaning.title: The Meaning of the Name
meaning.description: Nagham (نَغَم) is an ancient Arabic word meaning "melody" or "tone". It refers to a pleasant and beautiful vocal sound, especially used in Quranic recitation. The name was chosen to reflect the beauty and harmony of music, connecting to the tradition of praise and worship through melodic sounds.

staff.title: Staff
staff.description: Stave with treble clef and interactive musical notes

piano.title: Piano
piano.description: Interactive keyboard with clickable white and black keys

guitar.title: Guitar
guitar.description: Guitar fretboard with strings, frets, and clickable positions

chords.title: Chord Builder
chords.description: Interface to build and visualize chords with different qualities

future.title: Future Features
future.description: The project is under development and plans to evolve to include:

items.scales: Analysis of musical scales and modes
items.education: Interactive educational resources on music theory

about.verse: Praise the Lord with the harp; make music to him on the ten-stringed lyre.

chordBuilder.title: Build chord
chordBuilder.formationDescription: Notes that form the chord (low to high), to display on the staff, piano and guitar below.

home.title: Notes and chords viewer
home.description: Study notes and chords with a synchronized treble staff, piano keyboard, and guitar fretboard. Free web app for music theory and harmony.

about.title: About Nagham
about.description: Nagham is an interactive app for notes and chords across staff, piano, and guitar. Learn the meaning of نغم (melody) and the project’s purpose.

chordBuilder.title: Build chord
chordBuilder.description: Build chords on the guitar fretboard with root, quality, and bass; see voicings on the staff, piano, and fretboard diagrams.
